steps.title: Find the slope of the radius to the tangent point
steps.content:
The radius connects the center $(-4, -6)$ to the tangent point $(-7, -7)$. Using the slope formula:
$$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-7 - (-6)}{-7 - (-4)} = \frac{-1}{-3} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Apply the perpendicularity rule for tangent lines
steps.content:
A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals. Since the radius has slope $\frac{1}{3}$, the tangent line has slope:
$$m_{\text{tangent}} = -\frac{1}{\frac{1}{3}} = -3$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
